sections.content: A MAYDAY RELAY is transmitted when a vessel aware of a distress situation determines that the vessel in distress is unable to send its own distress call, or when no coast station has acknowledged a distress call. Any vessel or coast station may transmit a MAYDAY RELAY on behalf of the distressed vessel.

sections.title: When to Relay
sections.content: Use MAYDAY RELAY when you hear a distress call that no coast station has acknowledged, or when the vessel in distress cannot communicate on its own behalf.

The MAYDAY RELAY format is: MAYDAY RELAY (x3) — THIS IS — [your vessel name x3] — The following MAYDAY was received from [vessel in distress] at [time] — then repeat the original MAYDAY message — OVER.

sections.prompt: When should you send a MAYDAY RELAY?

options.text: Whenever you hear any MAYDAY call
When a vessel in distress cannot communicate or no coast station has acknowledged
Only when instructed by a coast station
When you want to practice distress procedures

sections.explanation: A MAYDAY RELAY is sent when the vessel in distress cannot send its own call, or when you hear a distress call that has not been acknowledged by any coast station.
